goal: Add AST deep scan + edge provenance INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS to match graphify, then verify performance comparison.

completed: scanASTDeep() — walks JS/TS files, extracts classes, functions, exports, interfaces, type aliases, modules via acorn or regex
scanCodeRelations() — now returns INFERRED/AMBIGUOUS confidence, supports --apply for auto-upsert
cm gi — provenance breakdown line (EXTRACTED: N, INFERRED: M, AMBIGUOUS: K)
cm init — auto-installs acorn + acorn-loose to ~/.cm/deps/
81/81 tests pass (3 new: AST scan, provenance, ge confidence)
Benchmark: 15/15 passed (cm wins on all measured dimensions)
issues: No benchmark before/after comparison for the new AST features specifically (benchmark runs on a fake project, doesn't test scan --deep on a real codebase)

next_steps: Optional: run benchmark on a real repo (e.g. code-mem itself) with --existing-repo to test AST deep scan at scale
Optional: update README/docs to document new commands and provenance
files: bin/cm — scanASTDeep (~200 lines), scanCodeRelations provenance, cm gi provenance, cm ge validation, cm init dep install, scan dispatch, parseArgs boolean flags, gl() help
tests/test-e2e.sh — tests 38 (AST deep scan), 39 (provenance + class/function types), 40 (ge confidence validation)
decisions: acorn + acorn-loose installati in ~/.cm/deps/ durante cm init (best-effort, non bloccante)
scanASTDeep usa acorn per .js/.mjs/.cjs, regex per .ts/.tsx (TypeScript non parseable da acorn)
--no-ast forza regex anche se acorn disponibile
Confidence: EXTRACTED (manuale ge), INFERRED (scansione con >3 match), AMBIGUOUS (≤3 match)
Nodi AST con ID formato ast__<hash36> per evitare collisioni
scan --deep upserta automaticamente nodi + edges nel grafo
